title: Differential geometry on the character variety
speaker: Julien Marché

proof_stages.label: Character varieties and trace functions
proof_stages.summary: For a surface or three-manifold M, the character scheme X(M)=Hom(pi_1 M,SL_2(C))//SL_2(C) is presented through trace equivalence and the regular trace functions f_gamma.

proof_stages.label: Goldman's Poisson structure
proof_stages.summary: For surface character varieties, Goldman's signed intersection formula for brackets of trace functions gives an algebraic Poisson structure, symplectic in the closed-surface case and mapping-class-group invariant.

proof_stages.label: Reidemeister torsion volume
proof_stages.summary: For well-behaved three-manifolds, Reidemeister torsion is described as a holomorphic volume form on the character variety, first by Haar-volume quotient for handlebodies and then recursively under two-handle attachment using the Hessian of a trace function.

proof_stages.label: Skein modules and the t=-1 character algebra
proof_stages.summary: The Kauffman skein module is defined from banded links modulo local relations, and at t=-1 its algebra is identified with the coordinate ring of the character variety via signed trace functions and the basic SL_2 trace relation.

proof_stages.label: Derived skein module as first-order deformation
proof_stages.summary: Taking coefficients in the dual numbers with t=-1+epsilon defines the derived skein module, and for a thickened surface its product equals the ordinary product plus the first-order Goldman Poisson bracket term.

proof_stages.label: Boundary restriction and Lagrangianity from exactness
proof_stages.summary: For a three-manifold with boundary, the skein inclusion of a boundary collar is dual to restriction of character varieties; assuming exactness of the derived epsilon complex, the ideal of the boundary image is closed under Goldman brackets, yielding the Lagrangian property.

proof_stages.label: Conjectural torsion-divergence operator
proof_stages.summary: The lift ambiguity for an element of the boundary ideal defines an operator on functions on the boundary image, conjectured to equal the divergence with respect to Reidemeister torsion of the Hamiltonian vector field of the vanishing function.

examples.name: Solid-torus torsion formula
examples.note: For the genus-one handlebody, the trace coordinate satisfies t=x+x^{-1} and the torsion changes from dx/x to dt/sqrt(t^2-4), illustrating how singularities occur at central representations.
examples.engagement_reason: he specializes to the genus-one handlebody, writes a diagonal representative, and computes the torsion as dx/x and then as dt/sqrt(t^2-4)
